title: Loud Party Disturbance
description: A resident calls 911 to complain about a neighboring apartment hosting an extremely loud party late at night, disturbing the peace.
initial_variations: I'm calling because my neighbor is having an insanely loud party. It's {noise_type} and it's keeping my whole family awake!
This is {caller_name}. I need to report a noise disturbance. There's a party going on and the music is {noise_type}.
There's some kind of party next door. The {noise_type} is unbearable. I've knocked but they ignore me. Can you send an officer?
I can't sleep! The people in the next apartment are making a racket with {noise_type}. This has to stop!

specific_details: I can hear every word of their conversation and the bass is vibrating my floor
They have a karaoke machine on full blast and people are screaming the lyrics
There's a live band playing in their living room, it's absurd

initial: I'm at {address_location}. You need to do something about this noise!
providing_info: Yes, it's {address_location}. They are in apartment 205, right next door.
waiting: I'm still at {address_location}. Can you hurry? The party is {party_duration} now.
resolved: It's over now, they finally quieted down. I'm at {address_location}.

initial: It's {noise_type}. {specific_details}. They've been at it {party_duration}.
providing_info: Like I said, {specific_details}. You can probably hear it in the background.
waiting: I can still hear the {noise_type}. The bass is shaking my pictures.
resolved: The noise has stopped now. I think they saw the police car.

initial: I'm exhausted and frustrated. I have to work in the morning.
providing_info: I'm getting more angry by the minute. I need someone to come now.
waiting: I'm waiting by the window. I just want this to end.
resolved: I'm relieved now. Thank you for sending someone.

initial: I knocked on their door twice, but they either didn't hear or ignored me.
providing_info: Yes, I even spoke to the building manager earlier, but he said he can't do anything after hours.
waiting: I'm done trying. I just need the police to handle it now.
resolved: I tried talking to them, but they were too loud to hear. The police got them to turn it down though.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and issue a warning. The party shuts down.

SEND_FIRE.message: Fire department arrives confused; this is a waste of emergency resources.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: An ambulance shows up for a noise complaint, diverting critical resources.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is bewildered; they can't help with a party.

DISMISS.message: The caller is told to handle it themselves. They are unhappy but no emergency exists.

title: Cat Stuck in Tall Tree
description: A distraught pet owner calls 911 because their cat climbed a tall tree and won't come down, fearing it's stuck.
initial_variations: My cat {victim_name} is stuck way up in a tree! I'm {caller_name}, and he's been up there for hours. I need help getting him down!
This is {caller_name}. I'm calling because my cat {victim_name} climbed the oak tree in my yard and he's too scared to come down. Can you send someone?
Please, you have to help! My poor kitty {victim_name} is trapped in a tree. He's meowing and I can't reach him. I don't know what to do!
I've got a feline emergency! My cat {victim_name} is stuck in a pine tree, and I'm afraid he'll fall. I'm {caller_name}, can you send a fire truck?

specific_details: The branch he's on is very thin and shaking
He's been up there since noon and it's getting dark
I tried climbing a ladder but I can't reach him

initial: I'm at {address_location}. The tree is in my backyard. Please hurry!
explaining: Yes, {address_location}. You can't miss the big {tree_type} tree.
assistance_on_way: We're still at {address_location}. I'm trying to coax him down with treats.
cat_rescued: He's down now at {address_location}. Thank goodness!

initial: {specific_details}. He's a fluffy orange tabby named {victim_name}.
explaining: It's a {tree_type} tree. {specific_details}. He keeps crying but won't move.
assistance_on_way: I can see him up there on the highest branch. He looks scared.
cat_rescued: The firefighter got him down. He's shaken but fine.

initial: I'm really worried. I love that cat like a child.
explaining: I'm trying to stay calm, but I'm scared he'll fall.
assistance_on_way: I'm relieved help is coming. I'll keep an eye on him.
cat_rescued: I'm so grateful! I'm holding him tight.

initial: He's meowing pitifully and clinging to a branch. He won't come down.
explaining: He looks tired and scared. His name is {victim_name}.
assistance_on_way: Still up there, but he's shifting a bit. I hope he doesn't fall.
cat_rescued: He's safe on the ground now, purring in my arms.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive but cannot climb the tree; they advise calling the fire department.

SEND_FIRE.message: A ladder truck arrives and rescues the cat safely.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics show up but have no equipment for a tree rescue; valuable time wasted.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control arrives but cannot assist; they suggest calling the fire department.

DISMISS.message: Caller is told to wait for the cat to come down on its own; they are distraught and feel ignored.

title: Parking Lot Fender Bender
description: A caller reports a minor car accident in a parking lot with no injuries, just vehicle damage, and the other driver is being uncooperative.
initial_variations: I'm {caller_name}. I just got into a little accident in the parking lot of the mall. Some guy backed into me and now he's refusing to give his insurance. Can you send an officer?
There's been a car crash, but nobody's hurt. It's just a fender bender. The other driver is being difficult and I need a police report.
I need to report an accident. I'm at the grocery store and someone hit my car while I was inside. They left a note but I want to report it properly.
This is {caller_name}. I witnessed a car hit another car in the lot and drive off. I got the plate number. Should I report this?

specific_details: The other driver is yelling at me and says it's my fault
My bumper is scratched but it's driveable
I have a photo of the other car's license plate
other_driver_desc: a man in a red truck

initial: We're at {address_location}. The accident is near the entrance.
describing: Still at {address_location}. The other driver is still here.
police_dispatched: Yes, {address_location}. I'm waiting by my car.
resolved: The officer took the report. We're at {address_location}.

initial: It's minor. {specific_details}. The other car is {other_driver_desc}.
describing: My car just has a dent, but {specific_details}. The other driver's plate is {plate_number}.
police_dispatched: I already told you, it's just cosmetic damage. I need a police report for insurance.
resolved: The details are in the report. No injuries, just a scratch.

initial: I'm a bit shaken but okay. Just angry about the situation.
describing: I'm getting frustrated. The other driver won't cooperate.
police_dispatched: I'm calmer now that you're sending someone.
resolved: I'm fine. The officer handled it.

initial: No, no one is hurt. It's just a fender bender.
describing: Absolutely no injuries. Just a scratch on the bumper.
police_dispatched: No injuries, I told you. It's only property damage.
resolved: Everyone is fine. It was just car damage.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive, mediate the exchange of information, and file a report.

SEND_FIRE.message: A fire crew is sent for a parking lot dent; they are irritated by the misuse.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics respond but find no injuries; they advise calling police for the report.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control cannot fathom why they are at a traffic accident.

DISMISS.message: The caller is told to exchange information themselves; they are upset without official documentation.

title: Suspicious Vehicle Circulating
description: A concerned neighbor calls about a vehicle that has been slowly driving around the block multiple times late at night, possibly casing homes.
initial_variations: I'm {caller_name}. There's a suspicious car that's been going up and down our street for the last half hour. It's a dark sedan, and I think they might be up to no good.
This is {caller_name} on Oak Street. I've noticed a van driving slowly with its lights off. It went by my house three times now. Can you send a patrol car to check it out?
I want to report a suspicious vehicle. It's a truck with a camper shell, and it stopped in front of my neighbor's house. I'm worried they're casing the neighborhood.
There's a car that keeps circling the block. I'm {caller_name}, and I'm home alone with my kids. This is making me nervous.

specific_details: The car is a black SUV with tinted windows
It turns off its headlights when it passes houses
I saw someone get out and look into mailboxes

initial: I'm at {address_location}. The car just passed by again!
providing_description: Yes, {address_location}. It's heading south on my street now.
all_clear: It left the area. I'm safe at {address_location}.

initial: It's a {vehicle_description}. {specific_details}. It's {suspicious_action}.
providing_description: As I said, {vehicle_description} with {specific_details}. I've never seen it before tonight.
all_clear: It moved on. I don't see it anymore.

initial: I'm on edge. It's so late and this is unusual.
providing_description: I'm getting more anxious. I don't want to confront them.
police_informed: I'll feel better once a patrol car is here.
all_clear: I'm relieved. It was probably nothing, but thank you for checking.

initial: It's a {vehicle_description}. I couldn't get the plate; it's too dark.
providing_description: The {vehicle_description} has {specific_details}. It's moving very slowly.
police_informed: I already described it. It's still around.
all_clear: I didn't get the plate, but it's gone now.

SEND_POLICE.message: A patrol unit is dispatched, locates the vehicle, and determines it was a lost driver.

SEND_FIRE.message: A fire engine responds to a suspicious car call, wasting critical resources.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: An ambulance arrives to a vehicle casing call; the crew is bewildered.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is utterly confused by the dispatch.

DISMISS.message: The caller is told to call back if the vehicle becomes more suspicious; they feel dismissed.

title: Found Wallet in Park
description: A good Samaritan calls 911 after finding a wallet on a park bench, wanting to return it to the owner but unsure how.
initial_variations: Hello, I'm {caller_name}. I found a wallet on a bench at the park. It has IDs and credit cards. Can you help me get it back to the owner?
This is {caller_name}. I just picked up a lost wallet near the playground. It's full of cash and cards. I want to do the right thing. What should I do?
I found someone's wallet in the grass at Riverside Park. I think the owner might be {victim_name} based on the ID. Can you send someone to pick it up or tell me where to take it?
I'm {caller_name}, and I'm calling because I found a wallet. It seems important, and I don't want to just leave it. Can the police take it?

address_location: Central Park, by the duck pond
City Park, on a bench near the entrance
specific_details: There's about $200 cash inside, credit cards, and a driver's license
The wallet is leather, brown, with the name 'Johnson' on the ID
No phone number in it, just cards

initial: I'm at {address_location}. I can see the bench from here.
describing_item: Still at {address_location}. I haven't moved in case the owner returns.
advised: I'll head to the station. I'm at {address_location} right now.
resolved: I dropped it off. I'm at {address_location} now.

initial: {specific_details}. It looks like it's from {victim_name}.
describing_item: Yes, it has {specific_details}. I'm not sure what else I can do.
advised: I wrote down the details you told me to include with the wallet.
resolved: The officer took it and will try to contact the owner.

initial: I'm fine, just trying to do the right thing.
describing_item: I'm a bit worried about the owner losing their wallet.
advised: I'm glad you could give me advice. I'll take it to the station.
resolved: I feel good. I hope it gets back to {victim_name}.

initial: The ID says {victim_name}. No phone number, just an address.
describing_item: The name is {victim_name} on the license. I didn't find a contact.
advised: I have the name now. I'll give it to the station.
resolved: The station said they'll look up the address from the ID.

SEND_POLICE.message: An officer arrives to collect the wallet, but a station drop-off would have been more efficient.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters are dispatched to retrieve a wallet; they advise the caller to go to the police station.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics show up but have no role in lost property; valuable time is wasted.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is completely irrelevant to the situation.

DISMISS.message: The caller is advised to take the wallet to the nearest police station; they thank you and proceed.

title: Late-Night Loud Music Disturbance
description: A resident calls to complain about loud music from a neighboring apartment late at night.
initial_variations: Hello? I'm {caller_name}. My neighbor {neighbor_name} is playing music so loud my walls are shaking. It's 2 AM!
This is {caller_name} from apartment {apartment_number}. {neighbor_name} in the next unit has been blasting heavy metal for hours. I can't sleep!
I need to report a noise disturbance. My name's {caller_name}. {neighbor_name} is having a party and the music is thumping. I have work in the morning.

specific_details: The music is so loud I can hear the lyrics through the wall.
It's been going on for three hours.
I can feel the bass in my floor.
They're screaming along to the songs.

initial: I'm at {address_location}.
describing_noise: Still at {address_location}. The music is still going.
dispatcher_advice: I'm at {address_location}. I'm taking your advice.

initial: I can hear {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}
I'm frustrated and tired.
describing_noise: I have a headache from the noise.

initial: {neighbor_name} lives in the next unit. This happens often.
dispatcher_advice: I think {neighbor_name} will ignore a note.

initial: Can you send someone to tell them to be quiet?
describing_noise: I just want them to turn it down a little.
dispatcher_advice: Okay, I'll call the non-emergency number then.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police are dispatched for a noise complaint. They will ask the neighbor to turn it down.

SEND_FIRE.message: Fire department is not for noise complaints. They are confused.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics arrive and find no medical emergency. Wasted resources.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control has no jurisdiction over noise unless animals are involved.

DISMISS.message: You advise the caller to speak to the landlord in the morning and provide the non-emergency line for future reference.

title: Ceiling Water Drip in Rental
description: A tenant reports water dripping from the ceiling light fixture, but no electrical sparking or immediate danger.
initial_variations: Hi, I'm {caller_name}. There's water coming through my ceiling in the kitchen. It's dripping from the light.
This is {caller_name} calling. There's a leak from the apartment above. Water is dripping through the ceiling and I'm worried about damage.
I need to report a water leak. My name's {caller_name} and there's a steady drip from the ceiling. I don't know what to do.

specific_details: The water is dripping from the light fixture in the kitchen.
It's been going on for about 20 minutes.
I put a bucket under it.
The ceiling is bulging a little.

initial: I'm at {address_location}.
describing_leak: Still at {address_location}. The leak is getting worse.
safety_instructions: I've turned off the main power. I'm at {address_location}.
resolved: Thank you for your help. I'm at {address_location}.

initial: Yeah, {specific_details}. I can hear {ambient_audio}.
resolved: I've noted all the details. Thanks.

initial: There's no sparking, but I'm worried about the light fixture.
describing_leak: I haven't touched anything. I'm keeping away from the water.
safety_instructions: Okay, I'll turn off the circuit breaker if it's safe.
resolved: Safety first. I'm on it.

initial: I knocked but no one answered. I think they might be out.
safety_instructions: I'll let the building manager know about the upstairs unit.
resolved: I'll contact the landlord about the neighbor.

initial: Can you send someone to shut off the water?
describing_leak: I just need the water stopped before it ruins my ceiling.
safety_instructions: Okay, I'll call the emergency maintenance line.
resolved: Thanks for the advice. I'll handle it from here.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police are not equipped to handle water leaks. They will refer to fire department.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive and shut off water to the unit, preventing further damage. They advise calling landlord.

DISMISS.message: You advise caller to immediately contact their landlord or maintenance emergency line and to stay safe.

title: Found Dog with Tags
description: A good Samaritan finds a friendly lost dog with tags and calls 911 instead of the non-emergency line.
initial_variations: Hey, I'm {caller_name}. I found a dog running around in the street. It has a collar with tags.
This is {caller_name}. I have a lost dog here. It's friendly but I can't keep it. Can you send someone?
I found a dog! Its name is on the tag. I'm trying to read the phone number but it's scratched. What should I do?

address_location: Near the intersection of Elm and 5th
At the park on Main Street
specific_details: The dog is a golden retreiver.
It has a red collar with a bone-shaped tag.
It's well-behaved and sitting with me.
The tag has a name 'Buddy' but the number is hard to read.

initial: I'm at {address_location}.
checking_dog_info: I'm still at {address_location} with the dog.
holding_for_owner: I'm waiting at {address_location}. The dog is calm.
resolved: Okay, I'll stay here until they arrive. {address_location}.

resolved: Thanks for the help. {specific_details}.

holding_for_owner: The dog is sitting by my feet, calm.
resolved: Pup is fine. Thanks for checking.

initial: I'm fine, the dog is not aggressive.
checking_dog_info: I'm keeping the dog away from traffic.
holding_for_owner: I'm in a safe spot on the sidewalk.
resolved: I'll stay safe. Thanks.

checking_dog_info: Can you look up the owner from the tag info?
holding_for_owner: Okay, I'll wait 20 minutes for animal control.
resolved: Got it, I'll call the non-emergency line if I need further help.

title: Blocked Driveway by Illegally Parked Car
description: A resident cannot exit their driveway because a car is parked illegally across it.
initial_variations: Hi, my name's {caller_name} and someone has parked their car right across my driveway. I can't get out!
This is {caller_name}. There's a vehicle blocking my driveway on {street_name}. I need to leave for work.
I need to report a parking violation. I'm {caller_name} and a car is parked illegally blocking my driveway completely.

specific_details: It's a red sedan with no license plate in the front.
I've never seen this car before.
It's been there for about 30 minutes.
I honked but no one came out.

vehicle_description: The car is right in front of {address_location}.
dispatcher_check: Yes, I'm still at {address_location}. Any update?
resolved: Thank you, I'm at {address_location}. I'll wait for the tow truck.

dispatcher_check: I've already given you the details. {specific_details}.
resolved: I've provided all the info. {specific_details}.

dispatcher_check: I gave you the description earlier. Red sedan, no front plate.
resolved: Yes, that's the car. Red sedan, dented fender.

resolved: Okay, I'll wait a bit longer. Thanks.

resolved: Thanks for dispatching parking enforcement. I'll wait.

SEND_FIRE.message: Fire department does not handle parking issues.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control cannot help with vehicles.

title: Neighbor Dispute Over Fence
description: Two neighbors are arguing loudly about a property line and a new fence, but no physical altercation.

Can you send an officer? My neighbor {neighbor_name} won't stop shouting. My name is {caller_name}. It's getting heated but no one's fighting.

specific_details: We're standing in the front yard.
He's holding a tape measure and pointing at the ground.
It's about a new wooden fence I installed last week.
We're both calm now but voices were raised.

title: Unattended Pot Smokes Up Apartment
description: A neighbor reports smoke coming from an apartment window and the smell of burning food, but no visible flames. The occupant is believed to be home but not responding to knocks.
initial_variations: There's smoke coming from my neighbor's apartment! I think {caller_name} left something on the stove. They're not answering the door!
I'm {caller_name} in apartment 3B. The unit above me, {victim_name}'s place, has smoke pouring out. No flames, but it smells like burnt food.
This is {caller_name}. I smell smoke in the hallway of my building. I think it's coming from {victim_name}'s apartment. I knocked but no answer.
Help, there's a lot of smoke from the apartment next door. I'm {caller_name}, and my neighbor {victim_name} might be inside asleep. It smells like something burning in the kitchen.

specific_details: the smoke is white and smells like burnt rice
I can see a thin haze through the window

initial: It's at {address_location}. I'm in the hallway.
I see {specific_details}. And I can hear {ambient_audio}.
smoke_investigating: The smoke is getting a little thicker. {specific_details}.
occupant_found: {victim_name} just opened the door. They're okay, just a burnt pot. {ambient_audio} stopped.
situation_controlled: The smoke is clearing out. It was just a pan left on the stove.

smoke_investigating: I tried the door and it's locked. I can hear {ambient_audio} inside. Should I try breaking it down?
occupant_found: {victim_name} just came to the door, looking confused. They had headphones on and didn't hear the alarm.
situation_controlled: {victim_name} is safe and the fire alarm is off.

smoke_investigating: I've been knocking for a few minutes. I have a fire extinguisher ready just in case.
occupant_found: I'm talking to {victim_name} now. They're embarrassed but okay.
situation_controlled: I'm helping air out the hallway.

smoke_investigating: There's still no fire, but the smoke is making my eyes water.
occupant_found: No fire at all. Just a burnt pan of rice. The smoke alarm is loud but no danger.
situation_controlled: It's all clear now. No smoke, no smell.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and find no criminal activity. A waste of resources.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters break down the door needlessly. The occupant had already put out the pot.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control shows up confused, asking about the 'smoking pet'.

title: Early Morning Construction Racket
description: A furious resident reports loud construction noise starting at 5:30 AM, well before the legal start time.
initial_variations: This is {caller_name}! There's some kind of construction going on across the street and it's waking up my whole family. Do something!
I'm {caller_name} and I need to complain. There's heavy machinery running at an ungodly hour. It's {ambient_audio} out there!
Hello? This is {caller_name}. I want to report a noise violation. There's a construction crew using jackhammers right now, at 5:45 in the morning!

specific_details: they have a cement mixer going and workers shouting
the whole building is vibrating

initial: I'm at {address_location}. The construction is right outside my window.
resolution_proposed: They're at {address_location}. I hope you're sending someone.

initial: It's a huge crew, {specific_details}. I can hear {ambient_audio} nonstop.
resolution_proposed: They've stopped now, but they were at it since 5 AM.

initial: It's deafening! I can feel the vibrations. It's definitely above legal limits.
noise_confirmed: Still loud, but they might be wrapping up. It's been going on for an hour.
resolution_proposed: Much quieter now, but they shouldn't have started so early.

initial: I looked at my clock, it's 5:30 AM. They started around 5.
noise_confirmed: It's now 6 AM. They've been at it for an hour already.
resolution_proposed: Almost 7 now, but they finally stopped.

initial: I tried calling the foreman, but he just laughed. That's why I called 911.
noise_confirmed: I recorded a video with a time stamp. I can show you.
resolution_proposed: I'm just glad you're taking this seriously.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and issue a warning to the crew for violating noise ordinance.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters are puzzled and can't issue citations. They advise calling police.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrive with sirens blaring, adding to the noise. Residents are furious.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control can't do anything about construction noise. A waste of time.

title: Loose Dog Running in Traffic
description: A worried caller reports a large but friendly dog darting across a busy road, causing cars to swerve.
initial_variations: There's a loose dog on my street! He's running in and out of traffic. Someone's going to hit him!
I'm {caller_name}, I just saw a big dog run into the road. I can hear {ambient_audio}. It's causing a mess.
Help! A dog is on the loose near the intersection. It's a golden retriever, no collar, and cars are honking.
This is {caller_name} and there's a dangerous situation! A dog is dodging cars on the highway. People are slamming their brakes.

specific_details: the dog looks like a Labrador, friendly, but scared
it's weaving through cars and seems panicked

initial: We're at {address_location}. Right in the middle of the road.
dog_sighted: Still {address_location}. The dog is running eastbound.
attempted_capture: Near {address_location}. I'm trying to get close to it.

initial: It's a big, {specific_details}. I can hear {ambient_audio}.
attempted_capture: I'm holding a leash and some treats. {ambient_audio} continues.
dog_secured: He's friendly! Just scared. No tags, but I have him safe now.

initial: It looks lost, not aggressive. It's just dodging cars.
dog_sighted: Still skittish. It's getting closer to the curb maybe.
attempted_capture: He's sniffing the ground now, calmed down a bit.
dog_secured: He's calm now, just thirsty and tired.

initial: I'm standing on the sidewalk, watching. I don't want to spook it.
dog_sighted: I have dog treats in my car. I might try to coax it over.
attempted_capture: I'm slowly approaching with a leash. He seems receptive.
dog_secured: I've got him by the collar (makeshift) and he's sitting with me.

initial: Okay, I won't go into traffic. I'll stay on the curb.
dog_sighted: I'll be careful. Thanks for the warning.
attempted_capture: I'm not chasing. I'm letting it come to me.
dog_secured: Good thing I was careful. He's safe now.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive but are not equipped for animal capture. They call animal control anyway.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters respond but have no animal expertise.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrive to treat a dog? Inappropriate use of resources.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control safely captures the dog and checks for microchip.

DISMISS.message: Call is dismissed, but the dog causes a minor fender bender later.

title: Water Flooding Intersection
description: A pedestrian reports water bubbling up from under the road, creating a large puddle that is blocking the crosswalk.
initial_variations: Hi, I'm {caller_name}. There's water coming up from the street. It's flooding the whole intersection!
There's a water main break or something! I'm near the corner and water is gushing out. No one can cross.
This is {caller_name} reporting a flood. The street is turning into a river. I think a pipe burst.
Help! {caller_name} here. Water is bubbling up from the pavement. It's creating a huge puddle and it's growing fast.

specific_details: the water is clear and fast-flowing
there's a geyser about two feet high

initial: It's at {address_location}. Right in the middle of the road.
update_provided: Same place, {address_location}. The water pressure seems lower now.

initial: I see {specific_details}. I can hear {ambient_audio}.
update_provided: Now the water is just a trickle. {ambient_audio} has stopped.

initial: No one is in danger yet, but cars are splashing through. It could get slippery.
assessing_flood: The water is about ankle-deep now. Some cars are turning around.
update_provided: Much better. The water is receding. Just a big puddle now.

initial: I'm standing on the sidewalk. I'm safe.
assessing_flood: I'm up on the curb. No risk to me.

initial: No ETA yet, but I hope someone comes soon.
assessing_flood: Okay, I'll let them know. The water is still coming though.
update_provided: Thanks for the update. The water department truck just pulled up.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive to redirect traffic and call public works.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive but have no means to fix a water main. They stand by.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrive but no injuries. An ambulance blocks traffic further.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is baffled by the 'water animal' report.

DISMISS.message: Call is transferred to the water department directly, and they dispatch a crew without emergency services.

title: Abandoned Backpack at Playground
description: A parent reports a backpack left unattended near a children's playground, which seems out of place and could be a security concern.
initial_variations: I'm {caller_name} at the park. There's a backpack just sitting on a bench near the playground. No one's around. Should I be worried?
This is {caller_name}. I found an abandoned bag at the playground. It's a black backpack, just lying there. I don't see anyone.
Um, hello? There's a suspicious bag at the playground. I don't want to touch it. It's been there for a while.
{caller_name} here. I'm with my kids, and there's a strange bag unattended. It looks out of place.

address_location: Sunrise Park, near the duck pond
specific_details: it's a black canvas backpack, partially unzipped
there's a strange wire sticking out

initial: We're at {address_location}. The playground area.
investigating_package: Still at {address_location}. I'm keeping my distance.
all_clear: Yes, {address_location}. The police have it now.

initial: It's a backpack, {specific_details}. I can hear {ambient_audio}.
investigating_package: Still there. {specific_details}. No one has claimed it.
all_clear: The bag was just old clothes and a water bottle. Harmless.

initial: It's a small, black backpack. Looks like a student's maybe. But it's been here for an hour.
investigating_package: It's definitely suspicious. I see a wire poking out of a side pocket.
all_clear: False alarm. It was just a school project with some wires.

initial: I'm keeping my kids away. We're at a safe distance.
investigating_package: We moved further back. I'm not letting anyone near it.
all_clear: We're all safe. The police handled it.

initial: Okay, I'll move everyone to the far end of the park.
investigating_package: Already done. We're by the parking lot.
all_clear: No need now. The scene is clear.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive, secure the area, and determine the backpack belongs to a student who forgot it.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters respond but bomb squad is needed; they wait.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control cannot handle suspicious packages.

DISMISS.message: Call is dismissed, but the backpack actually contains a homemade science project with a timer, causing panic later.
